text: Altitude training improves maximum oxygen uptake compared to training at lower altitude, with a moderate effect size.
Altitude training increases hemoglobin levels compared to training at lower altitude.
The live-high train-low (Hi-Lo) regime produces better improvements in aerobic capacity than other altitude training approaches.
An altitude of approximately 2500 meters is optimal for altitude training to improve aerobic capacity.
A training cycle of approximately three weeks is the recommended duration for altitude training camps to improve aerobic capacity.
The optimal altitude training protocol combines living at ~2500 m altitude with training at lower altitude for approximately three weeks.
specific_value: ~2500 m elevation, ~3 weeks duration, Hi-Lo regime
